rationale: Keep this as a non-public prototype: deterministic architecture evidence and all blocking admission answers pass, while the existing public packet supports only a market hypothesis and the custom model remains outside the Expanso boundary.

answers.answer: It proves that an edge operator can retain synthetic raw-media payloads locally while a modeled Expanso policy escalates only selected evidence and explicit model-adapter failures.
It links Filter Severity, Content Splitting, and Fan-Out as primitives instead of replacing them, and the same policy package is replayed against a bounded utility-damage fixture.
Expanso is central to local event shaping, allowlisting, escalation policy, and routing; the custom model owns inference, labels, scores, and evidence extraction.
The prototype labels Expanso policy, the camera/model adapter, custom inference, and external capture, receiving, and control-loop systems as separate components.
The core replay is local TypeScript with checked-in JSON and requires neither credentials, paid APIs, nor network access after dependencies are installed.
A pinned generator and deterministic seed produce two synthetic scenario packs and byte-stable expected checkpoints.
The checkpoints expose selected-evidence egress, raw-media retention, below-threshold local retention, and CUSTOM_MODEL_UNAVAILABLE at the custom-model boundary.
The 30-second oracle names the local-versus-emitted decision and points directly to the decision, rawMediaBytesMoved, and cloudEvent fields.
After repository prerequisites, one offline command validates the source, runs the policy, and compares exact checkpoint bytes.
The data-rights declaration binds generated synthetic descriptors and checkpoints to the repository policy, exact generator, seed, fixture digest, and allowed redistribution verdict.
Every byte count, threshold, score, and total is an exact synthetic fixture input or deterministic replay calculation; the result excludes accuracy, adoption, and product-execution claims.
Distinct producer and verifier lane assignments cover technical replay and editorial claim checks. This artifact proves configuration separation only; it does not attest independent execution.

valueOracle.summary: Keep raw media at the edge; emit only selected evidence or an explicit custom-model boundary failure.
